description:
**Milestone:** Operational Hardening

Production readiness: CRDT document compaction (prevent unbounded growth), sync conflict diagnostics, reconnection resilience after network partitions, monitoring and alerting for sync health, and documentation for multi-instance deployment.

**Files to Modify:**
- libs/crdt/src/maintenance.ts
- apps/server/src/services/crdt-sync-service.ts
- docs/dev/distributed-sync.md
- docs/infra/multi-instance-deployment.md

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Full compaction diagnostics: document size tracking, compaction history, and alerting when docs exceed size thresholds
- [ ] Sync health metrics exposed via /api/health/detailed
- [ ] Reconnection after network partition replays queued changes correctly
- [ ] Partition detection via heartbeat TTL with UI indicator showing 'Sync disconnected since [time]'
- [ ] Post-reconnection audit checks for dual-claimed features and resolves by instance priority
- [ ] Alert when peer is unreachable for > peerTtlMs
- [ ] Deploy-drain procedure documented: sync server extraction into sidecar container planned for future (accept in-process for now)
- [ ] Trust model documentation: Tailscale transport security, instanceId attribution, authority tiers roadmap
- [ ] Document Automerge.getHistory(doc) availability for time-travel debugging in distributed-sync docs
- [ ] Deployment guide for multi-instance setup on Tailscale
- [ ] Runbook for common sync issues (split-brain, stuck peers, doc corruption, partition recovery)

**Complexity:** medium

**Guardrails:** If this phase involves new or changed behavior, include tests that verify correctness. If this phase adds, removes, or changes a user-facing feature, API endpoint, configuration option, or service, update the relevant documentation in `docs/` to reflect those changes.
title: Compaction, Diagnostics, and Reconnection
